title: C# | SonarQube Cloud Documentation
content:
# C#

## [Supported versions](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/csharp/#supported-versions "Supported versions")

The level of support for a language is defined as follows:

- Fully supported: Analysis will complete. All the language features are understood and examined.
- Supported: Most language features are understood and examined but the version includes unsupported features. Analysis might break or provide incomplete results.

C#1 to C#13: Fully supported

## [Supported frameworks and tools](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/csharp/#supported-languages-and-tools "Supported frameworks and tools ")

ASP.NET MVC, ASP.NET Core MVC

## [Language-specific properties](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/csharp/#language-specific-properties "Language-specific properties")

Discover and update the C#-specific [properties](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/analysis-parameters/ "properties") in the project **Administration** > **General Settings** > **Languages** > **C#**.

### [Analyze generated code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/csharp/#analyze-generated-code "Analyze generated code")

To analyze tool-generated code (e.g. WCF code generated by `SvcUtil.exe`, protobuf code generated by `protoc`, Swagger client code generated by `NSwag`) for a specific C# project, enable the "Analyze generated code" setting inside **Project > Administration > General Settings > C#**. By default, tool-generated code files are skipped during analysis.

The detection of generated code is based on the file name, special comments, and attributes. The currently recognized values are in [GeneratedCodeRecognizer.cs](https://github.com/SonarSource/sonar-dotnet/blob/master/analyzers/src/SonarAnalyzer.Core/Syntax/Utilities/GeneratedCodeRecognizer.cs "GeneratedCodeRecognizer.cs").

When a `Generated` comment is present in the file, SonarQube ignores the *entire* *file*, even if only parts of it were generated. It's possible to enable or disable analysis of *files containing generated code* at the project level in *Your project* > **Administration** > **General Settings** > **Languages** > *Your language* > **Analyze generated code**.

## [Scanner compatibility](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/csharp/#scanner-compatibility "Scanner compatibility")

To analyze C# code, you need to use the [SonarScanner for .NET](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarscanner-for-dotnet/introduction/ "SonarScanner for .NET") version 4.x or newer.

## [Exclusions](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/csharp/#exclusions "Exclusions")

Files to be excluded should be set in the project configuration. Excluded files are still going to be analyzed during the compilation and the results will be filtered according to the exclusion settings. Details about inclusions and exclusions can be found on the [Analysis Scope](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/setting-analysis-scope/introduction/ "Analysis Scope") page.

As an alternative, a `.editorconfig` file can be used to disable the analysis for a specific rule on a file or directory. This can solve performance problems on large files.

`[Path/File.cs]   dotnet_diagnostic.Sxxx.severity = none`

## [Related pages](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/csharp/#related-pages "Related pages")

- [Investigating the performance of .NET Analysis](https://community.sonarsource.com/t/the-sonarsource-guide-for-investigating-the-performance-of-net-analysis/47279 "Investigating the performance of .NET Analysis")

- [Excluding External Roslyn Issues](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/external-analyzer-reports/ "Excluding External Roslyn Issues") (See "Notes on external .NET issues")
- [Test coverage](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/c-c-objective-c-test-coverage/ "Test coverage") (Visual Studio Code Coverage, dotCover, OpenCover, Coverlet, Altcover, VSTest)
- [SonarScanner for .NET](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarscanner-for-dotnet/introduction/ "SonarScanner for .NET")
- [SonarScanner for Azure DevOps](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarcloud-extension-for-azure-devops/ "SonarScanner for Azure DevOps")
- [Analysis of product projects vs. test projects](https://github.com/SonarSource/sonar-scanner-msbuild/wiki/Analysis-of-product-projects-vs.-test-projects "Analysis of product projects vs. test projects")
- [.NET test coverage](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/dotnet-test-coverage/ " .NET test coverage")
